name: Roborock vacuum / mop
short_description: Autonomous robot vacuum and mop that maps the home with LiDAR and AI vision to clean floors on its own.
long_description: Roborock's robot vacuum-mops are wheeled domestic robots that build a live map of the home using laser (LiDAR) and camera-based perception, then plan cleaning routes, recognize and avoid obstacles, and return to a self-emptying dock. First shipping a LiDAR-navigated model in 2016, the line is a mass-market case study in physical AI for daily life: a familiar appliance that quietly senses, maps, plans, and acts in a shared human space with minimal supervision. Later models add AI obstacle recognition for hundreds of object types and even a deployable robotic arm, pushing the household appliance toward a more general manipulating agent.

images.caption: A Roborock robot vacuum docked on its charging base.
images.alt_text: A round black-and-white Roborock robot vacuum cleaner sitting on its charging dock on a hardwood floor.

sources.title: About Roborock
sources.access_notes: Official company page covering founding (2014) and the 2016 LiDAR navigation milestone.

sources.title: Roborock
sources.access_notes: Background on company history, LiDAR mass production in 2016, and AI obstacle avoidance.

interactions.title: Steers around the dog's accident in the dark
interactions.description: Mid-clean it spots a pet mess, a charging cable, or a stray shoe and routes around it instead of smearing it across the room, calling out what it saw in the app afterward.

interactions.title: Picks your socks up off the floor
interactions.description: On the Saros Z70, a folding five-axis arm flags light objects under 300g — socks, sandals, small dog toys — while it vacuums, then circles back to lift each one into a chosen spot and clean the floor underneath.

interactions.title: A rolling pet cam you can talk through
interactions.description: From your phone you drive the robot around the empty house, watch the live camera feed, and speak to an anxious dog through its onboard speaker and mic to settle it down.

interactions.title: The cat commandeers it as a slow taxi
interactions.description: Cats discover the docked robot is a warm moving platform and ride it around the living room; the clips rack up millions of views, with behaviorists warning about long fur near the moving parts.
interactions.source_label: Newsweek — cat riding a robot vacuum

interactions.title: Owners root it to cut the cloud out
interactions.description: Privacy-minded users root the robot and install Valetudo, a community 'brain parasite' that keeps the vendor's navigation but runs the whole thing locally — no account, no cloud upload, no in-app upsells.

interactions.title: Go find the cat
interactions.description: Ask it to patrol and locate a specific pet by name, leaning on the same camera, obstacle-recognition map, and two-way audio it already uses for remote viewing, so you can check on a hiding animal from the office.

interactions.title: Ten-minute pre-guest tidy
interactions.description: Before visitors arrive, one tap sends it on a pickup pass — the arm clears stray socks and dog toys into their bins and it mops the entry — using the object-pickup and route planning the Z70 already ships with.
